approach.heading: Design that starts with how a room is lived in, not how it photographs.

0.title: Listen
0.body: We start with how you actually live — the routines, the clutter points, the rooms you avoid.

1.title: Draft
1.body: Spatial plans, material boards, and lighting studies, refined together until the layout feels obvious.

2.title: Build
2.body: We manage trades, sourcing, and installation, so the only thing you do is watch it come together.

statement.quote: A well-designed room shouldn't announce itself. It should just make sense the moment you walk in — and keep making sense five years later.

0.title: Full Interior Design
0.body: End-to-end design and project management, from first sketch to final styling.

1.title: Space Planning
1.body: Layouts that resolve flow, storage, and light before a single finish is chosen.

2.title: Renovation Direction
2.body: On-site coordination with architects and trades to keep a renovation on design.

3.title: Furniture & Sourcing
3.body: Curated sourcing across vintage, custom, and ready-made pieces within your budget.

light.title: Light
light.body: Every room begins with how light moves through it, hour by hour.

material.title: Material
material.body: Textures chosen to age well, not just to photograph well on day one.

form.title: Form
form.body: Layouts that resolve flow and storage before a single finish is picked.

detail.title: Detail
detail.body: The small, considered decisions that make a space feel finished.

craft.title: Craft
craft.body: Nothing off-the-shelf without a reason — every piece earns its place.

visualization.title: 3D Modeling & AI Visualization
visualization.body: See a room restyled before a single piece of furniture moves. Upload a photo and preview it staged in your chosen style.

architecture.title: Interior & Exterior Architecture
architecture.body: Spatial and structural planning that treats a building's exterior and interior as one coherent design problem.

exhibition.title: Exhibition Design
exhibition.body: Temporary and permanent exhibition spaces designed for flow, storytelling, and brand presence.

branding.title: Branding
branding.body: Identity and environmental branding that extends a company's visual language into physical space.

cta.heading: Have a space that's not working yet?

footer.tagline: An interior design studio crafting considered, livable spaces — from first sketch to final styling.

story.heading: We started Space Design Group because most renovations don't fail at the design stage — they fail at the handoff.
story.body: Space Design Group was founded to close that gap: one team carrying a project from first floor plan through final styling, so nothing gets lost in translation between designer, contractor, and client. Over the years that's meant working across full home renovations, single-room refreshes, and commercial fit-outs — always with the same approach: understand how a space gets used before deciding how it should look.

philosophy.heading: Good design disappears into daily life.
philosophy.body: We measure a finished room by how it holds up a year in, not how it photographs on handover day. That means prioritizing storage that actually gets used, materials that age well, and layouts that don't need a redesign the moment life changes around them.

contact.heading: Let's talk about your space
contact.intro: Reach out directly, or use the form and we'll get back to you within two business days.

form.name: Name
form.descriptionPlaceholder: What are you hoping to change, and what's prompting the project now?

servicesPage.heading: What we take on
servicesPage.intro: Four ways to work with us, from a single consultation to a full home or commercial fit-out.

0.quote: They asked more questions about how we actually live than any designer we'd talked to before. The result is the first home that's felt finished the moment we moved back in.

1.quote: We needed our office redesigned without shutting the studio down for weeks. Space Design Group phased the whole thing around our deadlines and it barely disrupted a single workday.

2.quote: I came in for a single consultation about a living room I'd been stuck on for two years. Walked out with a plan I actually executed in a weekend.

3.quote: Hartwell House had so much original character we were scared to touch it. They worked with the building instead of against it, which is exactly what we needed.
